title: Has anybody played the video games or Super Robot Wars
display_title: Has anybody played the video games or Super Robot Wars
wikitext:
[]: Has anybody played the video games or Super Robot Wars?             Quote Post by Rei_Rocks » Fri Jul 21, 2006 2:45 pm If so how good are they? For those that don't know Super Robot Wars has the Evangelion characters.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 21, 2006 2:48 pm Reichu has already suffered for our sins, and said " Suffering through GOS should be considered a rite of passage for NGE geeks "

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Fri Jul 21, 2006 4:58 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Reichu has already suffered for our sins, and said " Suffering through GOS should be considered a rite of passage for NGE geeks "

[]:             Quote Post by Alfonso » Fri Jul 21, 2006 5:28 pm *has taken the rite of passage* *actually enjoyed it, especially the Mana ending* :< Just cause she was hot and half naked and all. D:

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Jul 21, 2006 9:55 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Reichu has already suffered for our sins, and said " Suffering through GOS should be considered a rite of passage for NGE geeks "

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Jul 21, 2006 10:36 pm I finished this rite of passage recently, as well. Should I be embarrassed to say that I actually enjoyed this game? I personally enjoyed the Asuka ending, but I've always been a Shinji/Asuka shipper.

[]:             Quote Post by System Shocker » Fri Jul 21, 2006 10:43 pm GOS 2 lacked fandub :(

[]:             Quote Post by Rei_Rocks » Fri Jul 21, 2006 10:44 pm I still wish it came ourt over here. Even though I doesn;'t sound like a good game by what I'm raeding.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jul 22, 2006 1:40 am Hunter21 wrote: Then I am not going to be an NGE geek.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Jul 22, 2006 2:02 am It's not that bad! Really! All you need is some patience and a couple hours' worth of free time.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sat Jul 22, 2006 3:09 am Actually it's not very horrible. It's not so much as a game as it is a special episode. It's a nice way to burn two hours, and while I can't quite see how is that essential for NGE geeks. It didn't really mutilate the plot like it could, but the story it tells is so sweet at times you get a toothache. I personally got the Asuka ending, which was very cute. In the end, Asuka and Shinji grow closer. They even hug, albeit Asuka not acting like she cares. Her eyes tells the viewer she does, though. I grew to dislike Mana a bit. It was hard to define her character-sometimes she's shy, and other times she's outright straightforward. I was also shocked to see Shinji and Mana in an Onsen without a male-female division. That must blow.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Jul 22, 2006 3:33 am Legend of the Past wrote: I was also shocked to see Shinji and Mana in an Onsen without a male-female division.

[]: Re: Has anybody played the video games or Super Robot Wars?             Quote Post by Ryousanki King » Sat Jul 22, 2006 7:03 am Rei_Rocks wrote: If so how good are they? For those that don't know Super Robot Wars has the Evangelion characters.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei_Rocks » Sat Jul 22, 2006 11:01 am Wow, I heard people talk about SRW but know one that actually played them. Were do you get them? ebay or something?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Jul 22, 2006 1:06 pm It occurs to me that, seeing as the Evas are not robots, the correct title of the game should be something like Super Mecha Wars. But I digress. As for where one find these things, I suspect eBay has something to do with it.

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sat Jul 22, 2006 1:29 pm I've played Super Robot Wars Alpha 3 on the PS2 but the fact that its in Japanese is a bit of a problem for me. I've also played the first GOF game and I got the Asuka ending, it wasnt too bad more of an interactive episode though, there wasnt really much to do in it.

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Sat Jul 22, 2006 1:48 pm I got the Asuka ending too, it was good. overall as a game it wasn't really good, but I really liked the camp scene near the end, it seemed much....evangelion :P

[]:             Quote Post by Shadow Blaziken » Sat Jul 22, 2006 11:10 pm SRW, from what I've seen of it, and what people have told me, is badass. We actually could've gotten the series in America. Banpresto (the developers of SRW) asked ADV (since they had the rights to many anime featured in it, like Eva) if they'd license it over here (at the time, the first one would be released for the Dreamcast). Unfortunately, ADV asked for a shitload of money, and they just said "screw it." Fortunately, we're getting the SRW Original Generation GBA games here. The thing is, the mechas featured are Banpresto's own creations. Some of their designs are influenced by mechas like Gundams and even Evas.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei_Rocks » Sun Jul 23, 2006 2:10 am WE"RE GETTING THEM!?!? SWEET!!! Plus I think the best name would be Mech Wars. Super seems silly to put there.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryousanki King » Sun Jul 23, 2006 8:15 am I tend to get mine at import shops, but I lost my old copy of @3. That was really the only one I'd imported, I played decently accurate emulations of J and a couple others. @Shadow Blaziken: Yes, SRW is the epitome of badass. However, you're getting one thing slightly incorrect - ADV asked for a shitload of money not because they were greedy, but because they would need it in order to license and domesticate most of the stuff. For example, Mazinger, Getter Robo, Turn-A Gundam, etc are all in it, with about twenty others (in @3 that is) that are unlicensed. ADV would have to license them from the Japanese owners, then find a dub crew and dub the voices. It's a lot harder than it seems and is, in a nutshell, licensing hell, which is why none have ever been released before. However, Atlus is indeed releasing the two OG games under the original title: Super Robot Taisen: Original Generation. The designs are indeed all original, but mainly they take inspiration from Gundam and from various super robot shows (it's fairly easy to see in mecha like the Alt-Eisen). @Rei_Rocks: Lol. Mech Wars sounds kinda flat and American. Remember, this isn't just two or three games, this is more than (literally) one dozen games, starting with SNES (or was it NES?) and the original Game Boy and moving their way up to DS, PSP, and PS2, respectively.
summary:
[]: Has anybody played the video games or Super Robot Wars?             Quote Post by Rei_Rocks » Fri Jul 21, 2006 2:45 pm If so how good are they? For those that don't know Super Robot Wars has the Evangelion characters.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 21, 2006 2:48 pm Reichu has already suffered for our sins, and said " Suffering through GOS should be cons
